title: Division by zero in getVirtualPrice when totalSupply is zero
reason: `getVirtualPrice()` explicitly returns 0 when `totalSupply() == 0`, so this division-by-zero claim is false.

title: Division by zero in _earnReward when volume equals st_fee
reason: This only arises from owner-controlled fee configuration or extreme parameter choices; it is not a realistic unprivileged protocol bug under the contract's trust model.

title: Owner can steal all funds via callWithData with delegatecall
reason: This is an explicit owner backdoor/admin power, not an unintended permissionless vulnerability.

title: Inconsistent debt calculation in _withdraw causes incorrect token redemption
reason: `steth_amount = amount * aStETH / getDebt()` is the intended proportional redemption calculation before repayment; the reported inconsistency is unsupported.

title: Owner can set fee_pool to any address including EOA
reason: Minting fee shares to an EOA does not lock them; EOAs can hold and transfer ERC20-like tokens normally.

title: No access control on critical Aave operations allows griefing
reason: `reduceActualLTV()` and `raiseActualLTV()` are both protected by `onlyOwner`, so the claim is factually incorrect.

title: Hardcoded protocol addresses create centralization risk
reason: Hardcoded integration addresses are a design choice and do not by themselves create a concrete reportable vulnerability.

title: block_rate initialized to zero, no rewards until configured
reason: A zero default fee rate before owner configuration is expected initialization behavior, not a security issue.
